title: Palolem & Patnem | The Best Beaches in Goa, India?
text: Hey guys, good afternoon. How's it going? Today is Wednesday, March 25th, 2026. Currently 85° F and 29° C, another beautiful, perfect day here in Indian tropical beach paradise, Palolem, Goa. What a day, what a scene. I love this uh curving bay. Definitely one of my favorite beaches in India. Been here a few times before. So uh as you saw they were having breakfast, that was John from London, but lives in Ireland with his family. So uh I was planning to go to that uh restaurant there, which I'd been to previous times here in uh Palolem, and so I was heading straight there, stepped into the place, and John was uh sitting there and recognized me and said hello, and so I joined him for breakfast, and then while we were sitting there talking, another viewer recognized me, so I want to give a shout-out to Blake from Seattle. So uh cool to meet a couple of viewers there. But, it actually doesn't happen as often as it may uh seem based on meeting three different viewers here now in uh Palolem in the past 24 hours. Another uh woman from England yesterday. But, uh this is a, you know, backpacker foreign traveler destination. When I'm traveling around the world, I can go for weeks without uh running into viewers. But, uh always nice to run into uh fellow travelers and get the chance to uh hear about where they're from and what their trip is, where they're going, their impressions, and everything. of uh Hey there. What's up? It's always instant uh connection when I run into viewers because we obviously have a lot in common being travelers and being in the same country, etc. Hello. How are you doing? Request to public. No, thank you. Good luck. No. All right. So, uh this video is not going to be a uh repeat of yesterday's video. But, it is going to be a repeat of a video that I filmed the last time I was here in Palolem in which uh I just walk along the beach. You get to the end down there. And then you walk up and over to another beach. And then it is a series of uh beaches getting over to Patnem. Patnem is a very nice and even mellower beach scene than here. It's a great walk. So, uh a little something to do for today without having to get too uh adventurous or go on any long drives or anything. but uh one of the nice things about Palolem here is that it is well situated to be able to check out other places. For example, Agonda Beach is not far to the north, and then the woman that I ran into yesterday, Dorinda from England, had mentioned a beach that I had never heard of before. Cavelossim, I think it was. And so, I checked that out. It is about an hour's drive north, and so I don't feel like spending 2 hours getting there and back today. But, that sounds like another good one to check out at some point. So, anyways, let's get down to that end of the beach. So, as far as other beaches that I've been to in Goa, I've been to Arambol, Benaulim, and Bogmalo, Palolem, Patnem, Agonda. And so, I would have to say that these two beaches are my favorites of the ones that I have been to. Arambol is a great beach and scene, but as far as just the beach itself, then I really prefer this more curving bay. At Arambol, it's more of just a straight beach. And I think it's kind of more shallow as far as getting in. You have to like go farther to uh get deep enough to really get wet and enjoy the water, whereas here it's really quite perfect. So, there are a lot of beaches along the coast in Goa, but as far as the ones that I've been to, then uh these are my favorites. So, let's do the walk over to Patnem. And so far, liking my new hat. I literally bought it 30 seconds before starting the video right there. 500 rupees. And so, it is a very nice and short walk from there to the next beach, Kolam Beach. Right here. Really, uh, cute little bay, but also not a lot of space. And then, you keep on going to the next one. So, it's maybe a 30-minute or so walk to get to Patnem, depending on where you start your walk from. But, it is a very nice walk. So, getting close to, uh, Patnem Beach. Not sure exactly how much further, but uh while I'm walking along and showing a little bit of the uh scene here, I thought that I would tell a uh pretty funny story. So, yesterday I mentioned that I went to the Andaman Islands in 2006. And I took the ferry from Chennai, India, 2 and 1/2 days over to the Andaman Islands. There is the Andaman Islands and the Nicobar Islands. The Nicobar Islands are where the tribal people live and you can't visit there or actually maybe it's another island off of the Andaman Islands, something like that, but pretty sure the Nicobar Islands are off-limits except for like research scientists or government officials or something like that. So, you go to the Andaman Islands, which are in the Andaman Sea, closer to Thailand than India, but part of India and fully Indian. And so, I mentioned that on the uh ferry, then you had a bunk included, 30 bucks for the entire journey, including meals and a bunk, and they put all the foreigners in the same area, and so we all ended up talking together. And then, once we got to Port Blair, the main city, then a group of us stuck together and went from there to Havelock Island and had a great time there hanging out and doing all kinds of things. I mentioned two British guys. Oh, so this says Colomb Beach. So, maybe that first little one wasn't Colone Beach. Not sure, but uh anyways, the two British guys actually were not on the ferry. So, this is the story that uh explains their story, which is a wild one. So, they were supposed to be on the same ferry that I was on. I met them after arriving, I think, at Port Blair somewhere. And then they ended up joining our group and going with us to Havelock, or else maybe I met them on Havelock Island, I forget. But uh they ended up being part of this group, but they hadn't actually been on the ferry. So, they had booked the ferry as well, but they missed it. So, I forget what time of day it left. It might have been later in the afternoon or evening or something. And so, they were coming from somewhere else and somehow managed to miss it, even though they had paid for it in advance. But they were determined to Interesting. Coconut shells left out to dry. Not a great beach here, but check that out. So, that must be Patnam there, and it is a nice one. So, they were determined to uh get to the Andaman Islands. So, they booked a flight for the next morning. But the flight left at like 5:00 or 6:00 or 7:00 in the morning, very early in the morning. And so in order to make sure that they didn't miss the flight after missing the boat, they decided to stay up all night so that they wouldn't go to sleep and potentially sleep through an alarm. And so they tried to stay up all night, but in the process they ended up drinking and smoking and partying, basically. And then they fell asleep accidentally at some point in the middle of the night and woke up and their flight was leaving in like an hour or something like that. And so they rushed to the airport but they don't get there in time and they missed the flight. But they were still determined to get to the Andamans and so they forked out once again for another flight leaving later that same morning and finally managed to actually get on that plane and get to the Andaman Islands, but it took them paying for a ferry and two flights before they eventually arrived there. And then the crazy thing is I ran into one of the guys, Dean later and I think twice. Maybe both times in Bangkok. Definitely one of the times was in Bangkok. Maybe the other time was later in Bangkok. So, this was in 2006. Oh, I didn't go to Thailand. Yeah, I just went to India in 2006. My first time to Thailand was in 2007. And I flew into Bangkok and so it must have been then a year and a half or so later, that I ran into Dean there. And then I think it happened again because on that trip I'll just tell you really quickly that trip in 2007 to 2008, it was for 8 months. I had all my flights booked in advance. I flew from Portland, Oregon to Kauai, Hawaii spent a month there then flight to Bangkok spent I think 7 weeks in Thailand that time. I had a 2-month visa in advance. And then flew to India for I think 3 months and then went overland to Nepal for 2 weeks and then overland back to India to catch my flight back to Bangkok and spent another month or so in Thailand and then after that flew back to Kauai and spent another month on Kauai Island, Hawaii. So, that was the full 8-month trip. And so, I flew back to Bangkok and I think it must have been then that I ran into Dean for the second time or the third time. So, uh pretty wild. All right. As you can see, this is a great one. It is definitely mellower less busy than uh Palolem, but what a nice clean beach and another great one for swimming. So, I am definitely ready to get wet.
